jokes.text: Hmm, I thought I was being kicked exceptionally hard lately.
jokes.setup: Marge notices she's been getting kicked more than usual during pregnancy
jokes.punchline: The delayed realization of why - baby kicking harder
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge understated observation about pregnancy discomfort, well-integrated to character

jokes.text: If I could be any type of shark, I'd be a tiger shark. - How about you, Milhouse? - I guess a nurse shark. I mean... Oh! Ow, ow!
jokes.setup: Kids discussing what sharks they'd be
jokes.punchline: Milhouse picks 'nurse shark' (weak choice) then gets hurt, proving his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse characterization - picks the least threatening shark then immediately proves why he needs nursing

jokes.text: Shut up. What am I, some sort of joke to you people?
jokes.setup: Sea Captain is literally performing for tourists at aquarium
jokes.punchline: He's offended at being treated like entertainment when that's his job
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about performative work - Sea Captain breaks character to complain about being treated like a character

jokes.text: Shut up. It's not that exciting.
jokes.setup: Kids go 'Ooh!' at new exhibit
jokes.punchline: Tour guide immediately deflates their enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Sea Captain's curmudgeonly nature in just five words

jokes.text: Hey, without oil, you wouldn't have your fancy four-wheel drives. You want to go back to two-wheel drive? Well, do you?
jokes.setup: Environmental message about oil spills
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain defends oil with the threat of losing 4WD - absurd priorities
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how people prioritize convenience over environment, though delivery is a bit heavy-handed

jokes.text: I didn't mean to steal focus.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain just went on an environmental rant during tour
jokes.punchline: Uses theater terminology to acknowledge his outburst
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-theatrical reference that acknowledges the character's tendency to digress

jokes.text: These sea lions survive by eating the leftovers of the various film crews that plague the continent.
jokes.setup: Nature documentary about Antarctic survival
jokes.punchline: Sea lions eat film crew leftovers - commentary on documentary intrusion
jokes.explanation: Smart environmental satire about how nature documentaries disrupt the ecosystems they document

jokes.text: Where do you keep the fish till it's done? Oh, they're well taken care of in our storage facility.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks practical question about fish during renovation
jokes.punchline: Visual reveal of terrible 'storage facility' conditions
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons visual contradiction between what's said and what's shown

jokes.text: Oh, I might have known. But I didn't.
jokes.setup: Bart gets stuck in giant clam
jokes.punchline: Marge's understated response to her son's predictable misbehavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge line - captures her weary familiarity with Bart's chaos in minimal words with perfect comic timing

jokes.text: Bart will be up and underachieving in no time.
jokes.setup: Doctor reassuring about Bart's recovery
jokes.punchline: Replaces 'overachieving' with 'underachieving' as Bart's natural state
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Bart's academic reputation while maintaining medical optimism

jokes.text: Now, you stay shackled to that radiator until you come up with some insurance.
jokes.setup: Hospital needs insurance information
jokes.punchline: Doctor literally chains patient to radiator
jokes.explanation: Dark healthcare satire with absurdist execution - exaggerates real insurance problems to comic extreme

jokes.text: Well, it's 11 a.m. on a workday, so he must be at that stupid bar.
jokes.setup: Marge needs to find Homer
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact knowledge of Homer's day-drinking schedule
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's resigned familiarity with Homer's drinking habits

jokes.text: I don't think he's here. Uh, hang on. Let me check. [GRUNTS] Nope. What you wearing?
jokes.setup: Marge calls looking for Homer, Moe pretends to check
jokes.punchline: After fake check, immediately hits on her with creepy question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe characterization - combines his loyalty to Homer with his inappropriate advances on Marge

jokes.text: Okay, five more minutes and I'm chewing my hand off.
jokes.setup: Marge chained to radiator at hospital
jokes.punchline: Specific time limit before resorting to self-amputation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation with specific time frame making it funnier than vague threat

jokes.text: I went to the video store to rent a movie for the family.
jokes.setup: Homer explains his absence during emergency
jokes.punchline: Visual of him watching inappropriate adult movies instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer lie/visual contradiction, though predictable setup

jokes.text: That monkey's wearing a hat.
jokes.setup: Homer watching complex dramatic movie
jokes.punchline: Only notices superficial detail about monkey's costume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer observation - misses all plot/emotion for trivial visual detail

jokes.text: Look, he's misspelling words. [SOBBING] Oh, she's so...
jokes.setup: Homer alternating between two completely different movies
jokes.punchline: Emotional whiplash from silly comedy to tearjerker drama
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition showing Homer's scattered attention span and emotional volatility

jokes.text: You just can't get a cell phone. Lindsay Naegle, Veriqual Cellular.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions needing a cell phone
jokes.punchline: Salesperson instantly appears with fake company name
jokes.explanation: Good Lindsay Naegle appearance with fake telecom company name parody

jokes.text: Can I get a phone that plays the Mexican hat dance? I insist on it.
jokes.setup: Discussing phone features
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific and insistent requirement
jokes.explanation: Good Homer randomness with the insistence making it funnier

jokes.text: I dance, I dance, I dance Around the Mexican hat I dance, I dance, I dance And that's the end of that Or is it? I guess I'll keep singing My cell phone appears to be ringing
jokes.setup: Phone plays Mexican Hat Dance as ringtone
jokes.punchline: Homer sings along, creating meta-commentary about the ringtone itself
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation where Homer gets carried away with his own ringtone, becoming self-aware about it

jokes.text: Barney, you ever notice how hard it is to drive with your knees? Why don't get one of those hands-free phones? It's the next best thing to paying attention to the road.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about difficulty of knee-driving
jokes.punchline: Hands-free as alternative to actually paying attention
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of hands-free technology as safety theater rather than actual safety improvement

jokes.text: Then I could give the brothers the black-power salute. Black power, black power.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about hands-free driving benefits
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate racial gesture reference followed by awkward demonstration
jokes.explanation: Dated and uncomfortable racial humor that relies on Homer's ignorance but doesn't land well

jokes.text: Was that Al Roker? His exuberance is perplexing.
jokes.setup: Witnessing Homer's enthusiastic saluting
jokes.punchline: Mistaking Homer for TV weatherman, noting his confusing energy
jokes.explanation: Clever random celebrity reference with 'perplexing' as perfectly clinical description

jokes.text: Uh, a lighter. - I weep for you.
jokes.setup: Asked what's plugged into cigarette lighter
jokes.punchline: Salesperson's dramatic overreaction to normal answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect salesperson hyperbole - 'weep for you' is hilariously overdramatic for such a mundane response

jokes.text: These days, everything from fax machines to coffeemakers can be plugged into your dash hole.
jokes.setup: Selling car accessories
jokes.punchline: Calling cigarette lighter 'dash hole' with straight delivery
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny euphemism but relies too much on innuendo without much craft

jokes.text: Even I think this is crazy.
jokes.setup: Salesperson selling increasingly ridiculous car accessories
jokes.punchline: Even the pushy salesperson thinks she's gone too far
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where even the character pushing ridiculous products acknowledges the absurdity

jokes.text: Chaka Khan, let me rock you Let me rock you Chaka Khan, Chaka Khan
jokes.setup: Homer's car loaded with gadgets
jokes.punchline: Random Chaka Khan song playing as he drives distracted
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference without much additional comedy value

jokes.text: Save me, Chaka Khan, Chaka Khan!
jokes.setup: Homer in danger, calls for help
jokes.punchline: Appeals to the singer from his car stereo for salvation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the random Chaka Khan reference into Homer's desperate prayer

jokes.text: Oh, I'll never mock the Coast Guard again. You Navy rejects are all right.
jokes.setup: Homer grateful to Coast Guard for rescue
jokes.punchline: Insults them while thanking them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - genuine gratitude undermined by casual insult in same breath

jokes.text: And I certainly can't drive to the store at work.
jokes.setup: Homer listing things he can't drive to
jokes.punchline: Reveals there's a store inside his workplace
jokes.explanation: Minor wordplay joke that reveals Homer's workplace has shopping opportunities

jokes.text: That reminds me. We gotta pick up my slacks at that dry cleaner in Shelbyville. Why can't you use the local dry cleaner? Uh, I didn't want him to know my size.
jokes.setup: Marge asks logical question about dry cleaner choice
jokes.punchline: Homer's embarrassing reason for driving to different town
jokes.explanation: Good Homer vanity joke - goes to absurd lengths to hide embarrassing personal information

jokes.text: Thanks for picking my friends up from the strip club, Marge. Can we stop for ice cream? Homer always stops for ice cream. We'll see. That always means no.
jokes.setup: Kids want ice cream, Marge gives non-committal answer
jokes.punchline: Kids decode parent-speak accurately
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent-child dynamic where kids understand coded language

jokes.text: Public transportation is for jerks and lesbians.
jokes.setup: Bart suggests public transportation
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarrely specific prejudice
jokes.explanation: Dated joke relying on casual homophobia for shock value rather than clever writing

jokes.text: Stupid walking. Thighs chafing horribly. No drink holder. I miss my car so much.
jokes.setup: Homer forced to walk
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly petty complaints about walking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from legitimate complaint to absurd car dependency (drink holder while walking)

jokes.text: I can't drive 55 because it only goes 38.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about walking speed
jokes.punchline: References Sammy Hagar song but about walking speed
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference adapted to walking context with specific absurd speed measurement

jokes.text: I let go of the parking brake. Ralphie, if you stop... I'll let you play with my gun.
jokes.setup: Police chief trying to motivate his son
jokes.punchline: Offers weapon as reward to child
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about inappropriate parenting from police officer, fitting Wiggum's incompetence

jokes.text: Maybe I should just keep walking instead of going into a dark, dreary bar. Get in here, boozy. You're late for your drunkening.
jokes.setup: Homer considers healthy choice over bar
jokes.punchline: Moe immediately derails with creative alcoholic terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moments - Homer's brief health epiphany vs Moe's creative alcoholism vocabulary ('drunkening' is perfect)

jokes.text: No. From now on, walking is my beer and feeling good is my hangover.
jokes.setup: Homer rejecting alcohol for walking
jokes.punchline: Reframes healthy activity in alcoholic terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - can only understand healthy behavior by translating it into drinking metaphors

jokes.text: Maybe this is a sign. Maybe it's time to get out of the alcohol business... give barber college another try. And this time, I won't join a frat. Who the hell am I talking to?
jokes.setup: Moe considering career change
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's having conversation with no one
jokes.explanation: Good Moe moment with self-awareness about talking to himself, plus backstory about barber college and frat

jokes.text: You see? I'm finally thin enough to wrap this thing around my arm.
jokes.setup: Homer showing off weight loss
jokes.punchline: Visual of measuring tape actually going around his arm
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about Homer's small achievement in weight loss

jokes.text: You see? Before I probably would've driven to the syrup.
jokes.setup: Homer getting syrup for Bart
jokes.punchline: Admits he used to drive for extremely short distances
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's laziness - would drive within his own house for condiments

jokes.text: Calm down, Stresserella.
jokes.setup: Marge stressed about family transportation duties
jokes.punchline: Homer creates portmanteau of 'stress' and 'Cinderella'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that captures both Marge's stress and her servant-like role in the family

jokes.text: But real chauffeurs have uniforms and licenses. You could get in a lot of trouble with the Livery Commission. To hell with the Livery Commission. You don't know what you're saying.
jokes.setup: Lisa warns about unlicensed chauffeur work
jokes.punchline: Escalates to fear of obscure regulatory body
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic absurdism with the 'Livery Commission' as ominous regulatory threat

jokes.text: Indeed. You take this teacher-evaluation test. I'll proctor. Read the directions silently as I read them aloud to you.
jokes.setup: Skinner and another character stuck in traffic
jokes.punchline: Turns traffic jam into bureaucratic school activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - can't escape his bureaucratic educator mindset even in casual situations

jokes.text: Look at those suckers in their metal coffins.
jokes.setup: Homer now walking, looking at cars in traffic
jokes.punchline: Calls cars 'metal coffins' - dramatic reversal of perspective
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - Homer now has superiority complex about his former car dependency

jokes.text: Bishop to queen four. We're playing dominoes. I said bishop to queen four.
jokes.setup: Homer playing games with old man
jokes.punchline: Insists on chess moves while playing different game
jokes.explanation: Good Homer stubbornness - refuses to acknowledge he's playing wrong game

jokes.text: Simpson, you're a menace. Oh, George, leave the boy be. Martha, I want a divorce. Oh, George, you've made me so happy.
jokes.setup: Old man annoyed at Homer's disruption
jokes.punchline: Wife thrilled at divorce announcement
jokes.explanation: Dark marriage humor - wife's joy at divorce announcement reveals unhappy relationship

jokes.text: You're the first one here. Please accept this free ticket to the 1939 World's Fair.
jokes.setup: Homer arrives at work first
jokes.punchline: Burns gives him hilariously outdated reward
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - completely out of touch with time, gives 60+ year old ticket as current reward

jokes.text: Walking, eh? Let me give it a try. Well, I'd better drag myself to the hospital.
jokes.setup: Burns inspired to try walking
jokes.punchline: Immediately injures himself and needs medical attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns frailty - walking one step is enough to hospitalize him

jokes.text: We touched hands. Then we had to wash the cooties off.
jokes.setup: Kids explaining why they missed the bus
jokes.punchline: Cooties prevention took priority over transportation
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - imaginary hygiene concerns override practical needs

jokes.text: A Peruvian fighting frog. Hey, I brought a Peruvian fighting frog.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains her show-and-tell project
jokes.punchline: Bart coincidentally has same exotic choice
jokes.explanation: Good setup for sibling rivalry with absurdly specific exotic animal choice

jokes.text: When will they stop? Come nightfall.
jokes.setup: Marge asks when fighting frogs will stop
jokes.punchline: Homer delivers ominous nature documentary-style response
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and tone - two words that perfectly mimic nature documentary gravitas

jokes.text: Yay! I wanna amble. I wanna saunter. Amble. Saunter. Stop saying things.
jokes.setup: Kids excited about family walk
jokes.punchline: Escalating vocabulary war that annoys Marge
jokes.explanation: Good sibling wordplay escalation with realistic parental frustration

jokes.text: He proposed to another hooker at the bus station.
jokes.setup: Marge explains why she needs to pick up Grampa
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Grampa's recurring prostitute proposals
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about Grampa's recurring inappropriate behavior, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: It sure has. You see this bulge back here? Now it actually is a fanny pack. Unh, no, wait. It's still my ass. But your point is well taken.
jokes.setup: Homer showing off physical improvement from walking
jokes.punchline: Realizes he hasn't actually lost weight in that area
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness joke where Homer catches his own delusion mid-sentence

jokes.text: I like to walk down the avenue Bust a move with Disco Stu
jokes.setup: Homer's walking song
jokes.punchline: Disco Stu joins in with his characteristic third-person speech
jokes.explanation: Good Disco Stu character appearance with his signature speech pattern

jokes.text: Top of the morning, ladies. Bite us.
jokes.setup: Homer gives cheerful greeting to Marge's sisters
jokes.punchline: Their characteristic hostile response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Homer's oblivious friendliness vs sisters' consistent hatred

jokes.text: Hi, Homer. I'm actor Steve Buscemi. The guy who got fed into the wood chipper in Fargo?
jokes.setup: Celebrity introduces himself to Homer
jokes.punchline: References his famous death scene from Fargo
jokes.explanation: Standard celebrity cameo with self-referential movie joke, competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Would we?
jokes.setup: Offered tickets to Independent Film Awards
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic synchronized response to boring art film event
jokes.explanation: Simple enthusiasm gag about unexpected interest in independent film

jokes.text: Oh, my feet are inside me.
jokes.setup: Homer hit by car
jokes.punchline: Anatomically confused description of his injuries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer medical confusion - anatomically impossible but somehow makes sense in his logic

jokes.text: It would've been worse if I hadn't been carrying this Bible in my crotch.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how he survived car accident
jokes.punchline: Bible as crotch protection device
jokes.explanation: Absurd religious/physical comedy - Bible as inadvertent protective gear in inappropriate location

jokes.text: Aah! Too hot. Too hot. I'm sorry. I'm sorry. Aah! Hot. Hot. Hot. Warm. Tepid. Cool. Cold. Cold. So cold.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to feed Homer soup
jokes.punchline: Temperature goes through entire spectrum from burning to freezing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation through temperature spectrum showing Marge's difficulty caring for Homer

jokes.text: The doctor says that soon my pelvis will actually be stronger and more beautiful than ever.
jokes.setup: Homer reporting on his medical progress
jokes.punchline: Doctor apparently promised aesthetic improvement to pelvis
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist medical humor - pelvis beauty enhancement as medical goal

jokes.text: Gee, everything always works out for you.
jokes.setup: Homer talking about recovery while clearly suffering
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic observation about Homer's 'luck'
jokes.explanation: Good Bart sarcasm highlighting Homer's obliviousness to his own misfortune

jokes.text: That's ridiculous. I don't like you. I mean hate you, hate you, hate you. I hate you!
jokes.setup: Homer accuses Marge of trying to hurt him
jokes.punchline: Marge's Freudian slip escalates to full hatred confession
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation where Marge's subconscious feelings surface through verbal mistake

jokes.text: I've heard that from coworkers, strangers on the street, even my own children. But I never thought I'd hear it from you.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Marge's hatred declaration
jokes.punchline: Casually admits everyone else hates him too
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - matter-of-fact acceptance of universal hatred with Marge as last holdout

jokes.text: Who's we? Got a mouse in your purse?
jokes.setup: Marge suggests 'we' should see a counselor
jokes.punchline: Homer questions the pronoun with absurd alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - refuses to acknowledge relationship problems, suggests literal rodent instead

jokes.text: She used to elbow me in the face.
jokes.setup: Apu mentioning forgiveness for Manjula's affair
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of physical abuse as backstory
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about domestic violence played too casually, lacks the craft to handle the subject well

jokes.text: Perhaps you feel that your husband sees you as less of a partner and more of a doormat? Nothing could be further from the... You could be onto something there.
jokes.setup: Counselor suggests Marge feels like a doormat
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately changes her defensive response mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good therapy humor showing Marge's instant recognition of truth mid-denial

jokes.text: There's Homer, Homer J. Simpson, and Commander Cool, a.k.a. me. That's us in a nutshell.
jokes.setup: Homer listing most important people to him
jokes.punchline: Lists himself three times with different identities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer narcissism - creates multiple identities for himself rather than include family on importance list

jokes.text: If Marge isn't happy, I'm not happy. And if I'm not happy, Moe is very happy. But for once, this isn't about Moe.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his motivation to help Marge
jokes.punchline: Reveals Moe benefits from his misery, then catches himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant relationship logic - Moe profits from Homer's marital problems, showing codependent drinking relationship

jokes.text: You mean, like give her full custody of the kids? Yeah, full custody. That's exactly what I... You're an idiot.
jokes.setup: Counselor suggests unselfish gesture
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstands completely, counselor breaks professional demeanor
jokes.explanation: Good professional breakdown humor - counselor loses composure at Homer's spectacular misunderstanding

jokes.text: You mean, like give her full custody of the kids? Yeah, full custody. That's exactly what I... You're an idiot.
jokes.setup: Therapist suggests unselfish gesture
jokes.punchline: Homer's interpretation horrifies therapist into breaking professional demeanor
jokes.explanation: Homer's misunderstanding so profound it breaks therapist's professional composure.

jokes.text: You wanna know how to make a peach crumble? Kick it in the groin. Ha!
jokes.setup: Discussion of peach crumble dessert
jokes.punchline: Bart turns recipe name into violence joke
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart wordplay joke - turns innocent dessert name into crude physical comedy

jokes.text: You can bring a bag of ice. Lousy Homer. I'll show him. Tonight, his beloved mock apple pie will have real apples.
jokes.setup: Homer dismisses Carl's contribution offer
jokes.punchline: Carl's petty revenge involves adding actual fruit to fake dessert
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist revenge - Carl's spite involves making fake food more authentic, subverting expectations

jokes.text: You got everyone in Springfield here for me. Honey, I couldn't keep them away if I tried, except for Flanders. He was mysteriously called out of town.
jokes.setup: Marge amazed everyone came to her dinner
jokes.punchline: Homer admits to excluding Flanders with suspicious excuse
jokes.explanation: Good Homer pettiness - can't have nice gesture without excluding his neighbor

jokes.text: I brought you me finest catch of the day. We lost a dozen good men. But it's worth it just to see ye smile.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain bringing fish to dinner
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of massive casualties for single fish
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Sea Captain's romantic gesture cost twelve lives, delivered with maritime stoicism

jokes.text: That's it, eh? Twelve men. Well, I've got some families to inform. Unbelievable... Nothing. Just a curse on your very soul.
jokes.setup: Someone questions the twelve deaths
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain's casual attitude escalates to soul cursing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Sea Captain escalation - from mundane death notification duties to supernatural curse

jokes.text: Jackson Browne. Oh, the Pretender. From my latest album. Oh, no. Just kidding. Here's one of my many classics. That I fixed with new words.
jokes.setup: Jackson Browne announces he'll perform
jokes.punchline: Fake-out about new material, admits to rewriting old songs
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity self-awareness about rewriting hits for special occasions

jokes.text: Except a place to live and free food. Why, you little...
jokes.setup: Homer praising Marge for getting nothing in return
jokes.punchline: Bart points out she gets basic necessities, Homer attacks
jokes.explanation: Good Bart logic ruining Homer's sentimental moment with practical reality

jokes.text: Unh, I'm so full, my control-top panel is in shards.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing how full she is from dinner
jokes.punchline: Specific undergarment failure from overeating
jokes.explanation: Creative physical comedy metaphor using specific clothing terminology for overeating

jokes.text: You're welcome. And now, Marge, we cooked, you clean. What? Ha, ha, only teasing. We've flown in the finest busboys from France... Uh, I mean, America.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests Marge should clean after her own party
jokes.punchline: Fake-out followed by accidentally revealing foreign labor
jokes.explanation: Good fake-out joke with economic commentary about service labor origins

jokes.text: And now to all my dear friends, I say, get the hell out of my yard.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing dinner guests warmly
jokes.punchline: Immediately demands they leave in crude terms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - fake sentiment immediately followed by selfish rudeness.

jokes.text: And now to all my dear friends, I say, get the hell out of my yard.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing guests warmly
jokes.punchline: Immediately tells them to leave rudely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer hosting - switches from gracious to hostile instantly once his goal is achieved
